name: Catholic
description: Popes, councils, sacraments, and saints — 2,000 years of the Roman Catholic Church.

questions.q: Who is considered the first Pope of the Catholic Church?

source.notes: Tradition holds Peter was crucified upside-down in Rome under Nero.

questions.q: How many sacraments does the Catholic Church recognize?

source.notes: Baptism, Confirmation, Eucharist, Reconciliation, Anointing of the Sick, Holy Orders, and Matrimony.

questions.q: Which sacrament is the central act of Catholic worship, recreating the Last Supper?
What is the foundational creed recited at most Catholic Masses?

source.notes: Originally formulated at the First Council of Nicaea in 325 CE.

questions.q: Which 1960s ecumenical council modernized the Mass and is known as the Catholic Church's biggest reform of the 20th century?

source.notes: 1962–1965 under Pope John XXIII (and concluded under Paul VI).

questions.q: Which pope called the Second Vatican Council in 1962?
Which pope, elected in 1978, became the first non-Italian pope in 455 years?

source.notes: Karol Wojtyła of Poland; canonized in 2014.

questions.q: Which pope shocked the world in 2013 by becoming the first to resign in nearly 600 years?

source.notes: Last pope to resign before Benedict was Gregory XII in 1415.

questions.q: Who succeeded Pope Benedict XVI as pope in March 2013, becoming the first Jesuit pope and first from the Americas?

source.notes: Born Jorge Mario Bergoglio in Argentina. Died April 21, 2025; succeeded by Pope Leo XIV.

questions.q: Which 1054 event split the Christian church into Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox?

source.notes: Patriarch of Constantinople and the papal legate excommunicated each other.

questions.q: Which pope launched the First Crusade in 1095?

source.notes: Called for a holy war to retake Jerusalem at the Council of Clermont.

questions.q: Which 313 CE imperial decree granted Christianity legal status in the Roman Empire?
Which French city housed seven popes during the 14th-century 'Avignon Papacy' (1309–1377)?
What 1517 act by Martin Luther kicked off the Protestant Reformation?

source.notes: Said to have been nailed to the door of All Saints' Church (Schlosskirche) in Wittenberg.

questions.q: Which 16th-century council served as the Catholic Church's response to the Protestant Reformation?

source.notes: 1545–1563. Reaffirmed Catholic doctrine and reformed Church practice.

questions.q: Which 13th-century Italian saint, known for his love of animals and nature, founded the Franciscan order?

source.notes: Pope Francis (2013) chose his papal name in his honor.

questions.q: What is the official governing body / sovereign entity of the Catholic Church called?

source.notes: Distinct from Vatican City State, which is the sovereign territory.

questions.q: Which language was the Mass conducted in for centuries before Vatican II permitted vernacular languages?

source.notes: The Latin (Tridentine) Mass is still celebrated under specific permissions.

questions.q: On which day during Holy Week does the Mass commemorate the Last Supper, with foot-washing and the institution of the Eucharist?
Which apostle wrote a large portion of the New Testament epistles after his conversion on the road to Damascus?

source.notes: Originally Saul of Tarsus, a persecutor of early Christians.
